title: hypochaeris aethnensis subsp. depressa
intro: Hypochaeris aethnensis subsp. depressa, also known as the Greek dandelion or mountain dandelion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of gastrointestinal issues, as well as showing potential in lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Hypochaeris aethnensis subsp. depressa is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
